content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Judy Anne Harper 
Vice-President -
Finance 
TCS Enterprises, Inc. 
10525 Vista Sorrento Parkway 
Suite 101 
San Diego, CA 
92121-2746 
Dear Ms. Harper: 
March 10, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-075 
You requested information on behalf of TCS Enterprises, Inc. 
pertaini~g to the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act 
of 1974.11. 
QUESTION 
If a major donor makes contributions directly to or expends 
funds at the behest of candidates or committees totaling less than 
$10,000 in a calendar year, does the entity incur filing 
obligations for that year? 
CONCLUSION 
A major donor committee is a committee that makes 
contributions directly to or expends funds at the behest of any 
number of candidates or committees aggregating $10,000 or more in 
a calendar year. An individual or entity which does not qualify as 
a major donor committee during any calendar year is not required 
to file campaign statements for that year. 
Major donor committees 
terminate at the end of the year, and must requalify each calendar 
year before they incur any filing obligations for that year. 
1/ 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


ANALYSIS 
The Act requires a person or combination of persons to submit 
campaign disclosure statements in accordance with filing deadlines 
when it attains the status of a committee as defined in Government 
Code section 82013. 
If all the contributions are of the type 
defined in Government Code Section 82013(c), an entity does not 
qualify as a committee and need not file until its contributions 
total ten-thousand dollars ($10,000) or more during a calendar 
year. 
See 1988 FPPC "Campaign Information Manual" (copy 
enclosed), page 8, for a discussion of major donor qualification. 
If you have further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
encl. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sandra L. Taylor 
Political Reform Consultant 


o 
TCS ENTERPRISES, INC. 
January 30, 1989 
Ms. Sandra Taylor 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Taylor: 
FEB 
v 
9 24 liN f89 
Per our telephone conversation today, will you please confirm 
in writing that if a Major Donor makes contributions directly 
to or expends funds at the behest of candidates or committees 
totaling less than $10,000.00 in a calendar year, that the 
Major Donor has no filing obligations for that year. 
As we 
discussed, our company, TCS Enterprises, Inc. and its Chief 
Executive Officer, Tom C. Stickel, would like a letter con-
firming these facts for our files. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely, 
~~A~:~ 
( ~~ce President -
Finance 
" 
10525 Vista Sorrento Parkway, Suite 101, San Diego, California 92121-2746 • (619) 452-8000
